english_translation: Every city is my native place; all people are my kinsmen.
author: Kaniyan Pungundranar
commentary: All the world is our village and all people are our relatives.

tamil_text: உண்டாலம்ம இவ்வுலகம் இந்திரர் அமிழ்தம் இயைவது ஆயினும் இனிதெனத் தமியர் உண்டலும் இலரே.
english_translation: This world lives because men do not eat alone, even if the food were nectar of the gods.
author: Kadiyalur Uruthirankannanar
commentary: Selflessness and sharing preserve human civilization.

tamil_text: ஈன்று புறந்தருதல் என்தலைக் கடனே சான்றோன் ஆக்குதல் தந்தைக்குக் கடனே.
english_translation: To bear and bring him up is my duty; to make him a noble man (Sanron) is the father's duty.
author: Purananooru (anonymous)
commentary: Nurturing is mother's duty, molding character is father's.

english_translation: Death and God are the same to those of excellent character.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: Moral excellence makes one indifferent to death, equating it with the divine.

tamil_text: நன்பல வேள்வி செய்து முடித்த அரசனைக் கபிலர் பாராட்டினார்.
english_translation: Kapilar praised the king who successfully performed many noble fire sacrifices.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: The poet Kapilar's praise of king who upheld ritual sacrifices as a mark of righteous kingship.

tamil_text: ஒன்று செய்தோர்க்கும் ஒன்றிலாதோர்க்கும் ஒருங்கு உடைத்தே செல்வம் ஆற்றும் செல்வமே.
english_translation: Real wealth is that which is equally available to those who do good and those who do not.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: A philosophical meditation on the nature of true wealth.

tamil_text: இருமூன்று ஆண்டினும் கழிந்த யாண்டினும் கெடுக்குவர் தம்மை கெடுப்போர்.
english_translation: Those who bring destruction on themselves will be destroyed whether it takes six years or more.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: A meditation on the karmic consequences of one's destructive deeds.

tamil_text: வாடா மலர்தலை உலகத்து நீடார் புகழுடையோர் நீடுவாழ் வோரே.
english_translation: Those in this ever-blooming world who have enduring fame are those who live long.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: Opening verse of Purananuru praising lasting fame over fleeting life.

tamil_text: வாழ்தல் உயிரின் தொழிலே செய்தவம் மாழ்கல் இலா வகை.
english_translation: Living is the work of the soul; penance is the unfailing path to that living.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: Equates righteous penance with the duty of the soul to live correctly.

tamil_text: பிறர்க்கு இனிய செய்தல் ஆற்றுவாரே ஆற்றுவர் அவ்விடத்து அவ்வியல்பே.
english_translation: Those who can do good for others truly can act rightly in all situations.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: Doing good for others is the foundation of true righteous behavior.

english_translation: Neither evil nor good comes from others; it all comes from within ourselves.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: The Sangam poet's statement on individual moral responsibility and the source of fate.

english_translation: Under a king whose scepter is straight, there is no sorrow in the home.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: Connects just governance directly with domestic peace and happiness.

tamil_text: நிலம் பகர்ந்த நீண்ட மன்னர் இலம்பகர்ந்தோர் இல்லாத பண்பு.
english_translation: Long-reigning kings who distribute the land have the nature that lacks nothing.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: Praises the redistributive generosity of great kings as a mark of righteous rule.

tamil_text: வீழ்ந்தார் மேல் வீழ்ந்தன மழை நீர் புகழ் வீழ்ந்தார் மேல் வீழாது.
english_translation: Rains fall on those who have fallen; but fame falls not on those who have fallen.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: A reflection that natural cycles revive life, but lost fame cannot be restored by nature.

tamil_text: ஈயாது வாழ்தல் புன்மை ஆகும் ஈதல் வலியார் செல்வம்.
english_translation: To live without giving is baseness; generosity is the wealth of the powerful.
author: Various Sangam Poets
translator_notes: Avvaiyar's declaration that generosity defines the powerful over mere wealth.
